zh.lifeSummary: 當Agnes Napper於1554年出生在英國薩默塞特丁爾時，她的父親James Napier of Punknoll25歲，母親Joan Anne Masters31歲。她於1570年與Sir Thomas Cross在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾馬爾費爾德結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1635年4月16日在英國多瑟特梅爾可姆 雷吉享年81歲，埋葬在英國多瑟特梅爾可姆 雷吉。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Agnes Napper于1554年出生在英国薩默塞特丁爾时，她的父亲James Napier of Punknoll25岁，母亲Joan Anne Masters31岁。她于1570年与Sir Thomas Cross在英国英格兰格盧斯特斯爾馬爾費爾德结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1635年4月16日在英国多瑟特梅爾可姆 雷吉享年81岁，埋葬在英国多瑟特梅爾可姆 雷吉。

nameMeanings.name: Napper
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of English or German   Knapper   or of German   Nepper  .

variants.name: Nopper
meanings: German: occupational name for someone whose job was to remove burls from newly made cloth, from an agent derivative of Middle High German nop(pe) ‘tuft of wool’. Compare English Napper .

variants.name: Napier
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name from Old French napier, nappier, nap(p)er(er) ‘one who looks after the napery or table-linen’, a derivative of Old French nappe ‘table-cloth, napkin’. In Middle English, Napier and Nap(p)er might also have denoted a maker of table cloths, though this sense has not been recorded.

English: alternatively an occupational name from Nap(p)er from an unrecorded Middle English word for one who makes naps, i.e. drinking cups or chalices (Middle English nap, Old English hnæp). Napper could also be a nickname from Middle English nappere ‘one who sleeps or naps’, though this is the least likely of the alternatives.
American shortened form of Polish   Napierala   or a similar name.

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Agnes Napper was born in 1554, in Tintinhull, Somerset, England, her father, James Napier of Punknoll, was 25 and her mother, Joan Anne Masters, was 31. She married Sir Thomas Cross in 1570, in Marshfield, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died on 16 April 1635, in Melcombe Regis, Dorset, England, at the age of 81, and was buried in Melcombe Regis, Dorset, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Agnes Napper est née en 1554, à Tintinhull, Somerset, Angleterre, son père, James Napier of Punknoll, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Joan Anne Masters, avait 31 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Thomas Cross en 1570, à Marshfield, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 16 avril 1635, à Melcombe Regis, Dorset, Angleterre, à l’âge de 81 ans, et a été enterrée à Melcombe Regis, Dorset, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Agnes Napper 1554 in Tintinhull, Somerset, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater James Napier of Punknoll 25 und ihre Mutter Joan Anne Masters 31. Sie hat 1570 in Marshfield, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom Sir Thomas Cross geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 16. April 1635 im Alter von 81 Jahren in Melcombe Regis, Dorset, England und wurde in Melcombe Regis, Dorset, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes Napper è nata il nel 1554 nel Tintinhull, Somerset, England, sua padre, James Napier of Punknoll, aveva 25 anni e sua madre, Joan Anne Masters, aveva 31 anni. Lei sposato Sir Thomas Cross nel 1570, nel Marshfield, Gloucestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì il 16 aprile 1635, nel Melcombe Regis, Dorset, England, all'età di 81 anni, e fu sepolta a Melcombe Regis, Dorset, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Agnes Napperが1554年にイギリスソメルセトティンティンハルで生まれた時，父James Napier of Punknollは25歳，母Joan Anne Mastersは31歳であった。1570年にイギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアマーシュフィールドでSir Thomas Crossと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1635年4月16日にイギリスドールセトメルコンベ レジスで81歳で死去し，イギリスドールセトメルコンベ レジスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes Napper(이)가 1554년 영국 서머세트 틴틴훌에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 James Napier of Punknoll(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan Anne Masters(은)는 31세였다. 그녀는 1570년 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 마스h필드에서 Sir Thomas Cross(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1635년 4월 16일 영국 도르세트 멜코브 레지스에서 81세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 도르세트 멜코브 레지스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes Napper nasceu em 1554, em Tintinhull, Somerset, Inglaterra, seu pai, James Napier of Punknoll, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Joan Anne Masters, tinha 31 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Thomas Cross em 1570, em Marshfield, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 16 de abril de 1635, em Melcombe Regis, Dorset, Inglaterra, com 81 anos, e foi sepultada em Melcombe Regis, Dorset, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnes Napper родилась в 1554 г. в местности тинтинулл, сомирсет, Англия, её отцу по имени James Napier of Punknoll было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan Anne Masters, 31. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Thomas Cross в 1570 г. в местности маршфиелд, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла 16 апреля 1635 г. в местности мелкомб регис, дорсет, Англия в возрасте 81 год и была похоронена в местности мелкомб регис, дорсет, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Agnes Napper nació en 1554, en Tintinhull, Somerset, Inglaterra, su papá, James Napier of Punknoll, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Joan Anne Masters, tenía 31 años. Se casó con Sir Thomas Cross en 1570, en Marshfield, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió el 16 de abril de 1635, en Melcombe Regis, Dorset, Inglaterra, a la edad de 81 años, y fue sepultada en Melcombe Regis, Dorset, Inglaterra.
